---
title: "9999_dev"
author: "Jagadeesh Puvvula"
date: "2026-03-23"
output: html_document
---

#multiple imputation linear regression
```{r}
lm_exp_mixtures <- function(dependent_vars, independent_vars, covariates, data,
                            include_sex = TRUE, include_cohort = TRUE,
                            log2_vars = NULL) {
  
  if (!inherits(data, "mids")) stop("`data` must be a mids object from the mice package.")
  require(mice)
  
  m <- data$m
  imp_datasets <- mice::complete(data, action = "all")
  
  # ── Log2 transform specified variables in each imputed dataset ───────────────
  if (!is.null(log2_vars)) {
    imp_datasets <- lapply(imp_datasets, function(d) {
      missing_vars <- setdiff(log2_vars, names(d))
      if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
        warning(paste("log2_vars not found in data:", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")))
      }
      vars_present <- intersect(log2_vars, names(d))
      for (v in vars_present) {
        if (any(d[[v]] <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
          warning(paste0("Variable '", v, "' contains non-positive values; log2 transform may produce NaN/Inf."))
        }
        d[[v]] <- log2(d[[v]])
      }
      d
    })
  }
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  
  dependent_list    <- list()
  independent_list  <- list()
  sex_level_list    <- list()
  cohort_level_list <- list()
  coef_list         <- list()
  p_value_list      <- list()
  ci_list           <- list()
  
  has_zero_variance <- function(df) {
    sapply(df, function(x) is.numeric(x) && (var(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0))
  }
  
  # ── Rubin's rules pooling helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  pool_rubin <- function(fits, param) {
    
    betas <- tryCatch(
      sapply(fits, function(f) coef(f)[param]),
      error = function(e) return(NULL)
    )
    if (is.null(betas) || any(is.na(betas))) return(list(beta = NA, se = NA, p = NA,
                                                          ci_low = NA, ci_high = NA))
    
    within_vars <- sapply(fits, function(f) vcov(f)[param, param])
    
    Q_bar <- mean(betas)
    W     <- mean(within_vars)
    B     <- var(betas)
    T_var <- W + (1 + 1 / m) * B
    se    <- sqrt(T_var)
    
    r    <- (1 + 1 / m) * B / W
    v_df <- (m - 1) * (1 + 1 / r)^2
    
    t_stat  <- Q_bar / se
    p_val   <- 2 * pt(abs(t_stat), df = v_df, lower.tail = FALSE)
    ci_low  <- Q_bar - qt(0.975, df = v_df) * se
    ci_high <- Q_bar + qt(0.975, df = v_df) * se
    
    list(beta = Q_bar, se = se, p = p_val, ci_low = ci_low, ci_high = ci_high)
  }
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  
  for (sex_level in c("all", "Female", "Male")) {
    
    if (include_sex) {
      if (sex_level == "all") {
        sex_datasets <- imp_datasets
        sex_formula  <- "+ sex"
      } else {
        sex_datasets <- lapply(imp_datasets, function(d) droplevels(subset(d, sex == sex_level)))
        sex_formula  <- ""
      }
    } else {
      sex_datasets <- imp_datasets
      sex_formula  <- ""
    }
    
    for (cohort_level in c("all", "HOME", "MIREC")) {
      
      if (include_cohort) {
        if (cohort_level == "all") {
          cohort_datasets <- sex_datasets
          cohort_formula  <- "+ cohort + center"
        } else if (cohort_level == "HOME") {
          cohort_datasets <- lapply(sex_datasets, function(d) droplevels(subset(d, cohort == "HOME")))
          cohort_formula  <- ""
        } else if (cohort_level == "MIREC") {
          cohort_datasets <- lapply(sex_datasets, function(d) droplevels(subset(d, cohort == "MIREC")))
          cohort_formula  <- "+ center"
        } else {
          stop("Invalid cohort level")
        }
      } else {
        cohort_datasets <- sex_datasets
        cohort_formula  <- ""
      }
      
      if (any(sapply(cohort_datasets, nrow) == 0)) {
        warning(paste("No data for sex =", sex_level, "and cohort =", cohort_level, "- skipping"))
        next
      }
      
      for (dep_var in dependent_vars) {
        for (ind_var in independent_vars) {
          
          # Remove stratification variables from covariates when applicable
          adjusted_covariates <- covariates
          if (sex_level != "all") adjusted_covariates <- setdiff(adjusted_covariates, "sex")
          if (cohort_level != "all") adjusted_covariates <- setdiff(adjusted_covariates, c("cohort", "center"))
          
          formula_str <- paste0(dep_var, " ~ ", ind_var, " + ",
                                paste(adjusted_covariates, collapse = " + "),
                                sex_formula, cohort_formula)
          formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
          
          # Check for zero variance in variables used in this specific model
          model_vars <- c(dep_var, ind_var, covariates)
          model_vars <- intersect(model_vars, colnames(cohort_datasets[[1]]))
          
          has_issue <- FALSE
          for (d in cohort_datasets) {
            issue_vars <- names(which(sapply(d[model_vars], function(x) {
              is.numeric(x) && var(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0
            })))
            if (length(issue_vars) > 0) {
              warning(paste0("Zero variance in ", paste(issue_vars, collapse = ", "),
                             " for dep=", dep_var, ", ind=", ind_var,
                             " | sex=", sex_level, " | cohort=", cohort_level, " - skipping"))
              has_issue <- TRUE
              break
            }
          }
          if (has_issue) next
          
          fits <- lapply(cohort_datasets, function(d) {
            tryCatch(lm(formula, data = d), error = function(e) {
              warning(paste0("lm() failed: ", deparse(formula),
                             " | sex = ", sex_level, " | cohort = ", cohort_level,
                             " | Error: ", e$message))
              NULL
            })
          })
          
          if (any(sapply(fits, is.null))) next
          
          if (!all(sapply(fits, function(f) ind_var %in% names(coef(f))))) {
            warning(paste0("Coefficient for ", ind_var, " not found in all models. ",
                           "sex=", sex_level, " cohort=", cohort_level))
            next
          }
          
          pooled <- pool_rubin(fits, ind_var)
          
          dependent_list[[length(dependent_list) + 1]]      <- dep_var
          independent_list[[length(independent_list) + 1]]  <- ind_var
          sex_level_list[[length(sex_level_list) + 1]]       <- sex_level
          cohort_level_list[[length(cohort_level_list) + 1]] <- cohort_level
          coef_list[[length(coef_list) + 1]]                 <- sprintf("%.2f", pooled$beta)
          p_value_list[[length(p_value_list) + 1]]           <- pooled$p
          ci_list[[length(ci_list) + 1]]                     <- sprintf("(%.2f, %.2f)", pooled$ci_low, pooled$ci_high)
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  results <- data.frame(
    dependent_variable   = unlist(dependent_list),
    independent_variable = unlist(independent_list),
    sex_level            = unlist(sex_level_list),
    cohort_level         = unlist(cohort_level_list),
    coefficient          = unlist(coef_list),
    p_value              = unlist(p_value_list),
    ci_95                = unlist(ci_list),
    stringsAsFactors     = FALSE
  )
  
  results$p_value_fdr <- p.adjust(results$p_value, method = "fdr")
  
  return(results)
}
```

#qgc_multiple_miputations
```{r}
apply_log2 <- function(data, log2_vars) {

  if (is.null(log2_vars) || length(log2_vars) == 0) return(data)

  # Check all requested vars exist in data
  missing_vars <- setdiff(log2_vars, names(data))
  if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "log2_vars not found in data: %s",
      paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }

  # Check for non-positive values which would produce NaN/Inf
  non_positive <- vapply(log2_vars, function(v) any(data[[v]] <= 0, na.rm = TRUE), logical(1))
  if (any(non_positive)) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "log2 transformation requires strictly positive values. Non-positive values detected in: %s",
      paste(log2_vars[non_positive], collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }

  data[log2_vars] <- lapply(data[log2_vars], log2)
  data
}


# --- Process all imputed datasets ---
process_imputed_datasets <- function(mi_data,
                                     n_imputations  = 15,
                                     output_base_folder,
                                     outcomes,
                                     chemicals,
                                     covariates,
                                     log2_vars      = NULL,   # <-- new: NULL means no transformation
                                     include_sex    = TRUE,
                                     include_cohort = TRUE,
                                     q, b, seed) {

  # Validate inputs
  stopifnot(inherits(mi_data, "mids"))
  if (n_imputations > mi_data$m) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "n_imputations (%d) exceeds available imputations in mi_data (%d).",
      n_imputations, mi_data$m
    ))
  }

  # Warn if log2_vars contains names not in chemicals or covariates
  # (outcomes on log2 scale would be unusual — flag it)
  if (!is.null(log2_vars)) {
    unexpected <- setdiff(log2_vars, c(chemicals, covariates))
    if (length(unexpected) > 0) {
      warning(sprintf(
        "log2_vars contains variables not listed in chemicals or covariates: %s",
        paste(unexpected, collapse = ", ")
      ))
    }
    message(sprintf("log2 transformation will be applied to: %s",
                    paste(log2_vars, collapse = ", ")))
  }

  # Create main output folder
  if (!dir.exists(output_base_folder)) {
    dir.create(output_base_folder, recursive = TRUE)
  }

  # Track per-iteration status
  status <- character(n_imputations)

  for (i in seq_len(n_imputations)) {

    iter_folder <- file.path(output_base_folder, paste0("iter_", i))
    if (!dir.exists(iter_folder)) dir.create(iter_folder)

    message(sprintf("Processing imputation %d of %d ...", i, n_imputations))

    tryCatch({
      dat_mi_i <- mice::complete(mi_data, i)

      # Apply log2 transformation before passing to qgcomp_func
      dat_mi_i <- apply_log2(dat_mi_i, log2_vars)

      qgcomp_func(
        outcomes       = outcomes,
        data           = dat_mi_i,
        output_folder  = iter_folder,
        chemicals      = chemicals,
        covariates     = covariates,
        include_sex    = include_sex,
        include_cohort = include_cohort,
        q              = q,
        b              = b,
        seed           = seed
      )

      status[i] <- "ok"
      message(sprintf("  -> Imputation %d complete.", i))

    }, error = function(e) {
      status[i] <<- "error"
      message(sprintf("  -> ERROR on imputation %d: %s", i, e$message))
    })
  }

  # Summary report
  n_ok  <- sum(status == "ok")
  n_err <- sum(status == "error")
  message(sprintf("\nDone. %d/%d succeeded, %d failed.", n_ok, n_imputations, n_err))

  if (n_err > 0) {
    message("Failed iterations: ", paste(which(status == "error"), collapse = ", "))
  }

  invisible(setNames(status, paste0("iter_", seq_len(n_imputations))))
}
```

#extract qgc pooled results
```{r}
pool_imputed_results <- function(base_path,
                                 n_imputations = 25,
                                 dfcom,
                                 model_types = c("nb", "boot")) {

  # --- validate inputs ---
  required_names <- c("all", "Female", "Male")
  if (!all(required_names %in% names(dfcom)))
    stop(sprintf("dfcom must be a named vector with names: %s",
                 paste(required_names, collapse = ", ")))

  # --- sort iter folders numerically ---
  iter_folders <- list.dirs(base_path, recursive = FALSE)
  iter_folders <- iter_folders[grepl("iter_\\d+$", iter_folders)]
  iter_folders <- iter_folders[order(as.integer(sub(".*iter_", "", iter_folders)))]

  if (length(iter_folders) != n_imputations)
    warning(sprintf("Expected %d iter folders, found %d.", n_imputations, length(iter_folders)))

  # --- get target files from iter_1 ---
  all_files    <- list.files(iter_folders[1], pattern = "\\.rda$")
  model_pat    <- paste0("^(", paste(model_types, collapse = "|"), ")_")
  target_files <- all_files[grepl(model_pat, all_files)]

  message(sprintf("Found %d files to pool per iteration.", length(target_files)))

  # --- helper: load object from rda ---
  load_obj <- function(path) {
    e <- new.env()
    load(path, envir = e)
    objs <- ls(e)
    if (length(objs) == 1) return(get(objs[1], envir = e))
    for (obj_name in objs) {
      obj <- get(obj_name, envir = e)
      if (inherits(obj, c("qgcompfit", "qgcomp", "lm", "glm"))) return(obj)
    }
    stop("No recognised model object found in: ", path)
  }

  # --- helper: extract psi1 estimate and variance ---
  extract_psi1 <- function(fit) {
    if (!is.null(fit$psi) && !is.null(fit$var.psi)) {
      return(list(estimate = fit$psi[[1]], variance = fit$var.psi[[1]]))
    }
    if ("psi1" %in% names(coef(fit))) {
      return(list(estimate = coef(fit)[["psi1"]], variance = vcov(fit)["psi1", "psi1"]))
    }
    stop("Cannot extract psi1 from model object.")
  }

  # --- manual Rubin's rules with edge case handling ---
  rubins_rules <- function(estimates, variances, m, dfcom) {

    Q_bar <- mean(estimates)
    U_bar <- mean(variances)
    B     <- var(estimates)       # between-imputation variance
    T_var <- U_bar + (1 + 1/m) * B

    # guard: T_var must be positive
    if (is.na(T_var) || T_var <= 0) {
      warning("Total variance is non-positive — check model outputs for this stratum.")
      return(list(estimate  = Q_bar, std.error = NA, conf.low  = NA,
                  conf.high = NA,    t_stat    = NA, df        = NA,
                  p.value   = NA,    lambda    = NA))
    }

    SE     <- sqrt(T_var)
    lambda <- (1 + 1/m) * B / T_var  # fraction of missing information

    # degrees of freedom (Barnard & Rubin 1999)
    # when lambda is effectively 0 (B ≈ 0), all info is within-imputation
    # df_old → Inf, so df collapses to df_obs
    df_obs <- (dfcom + 1) / (dfcom + 3) * dfcom * (1 - lambda)

    df <- if (lambda < .Machine$double.eps || is.na(lambda)) {
      # no between-imputation variance: use observed df directly
      df_obs
    } else {
      df_old <- (m - 1) / lambda^2
      (df_old * df_obs) / (df_old + df_obs)
    }

    # final guard: df must be a finite positive number
    if (is.na(df) || !is.finite(df) || df <= 0) {
      warning(sprintf("Computed df is invalid (%.4f) — falling back to df = dfcom.", df))
      df <- dfcom
    }

    t_stat <- Q_bar / SE
    p_val  <- 2 * pt(abs(t_stat), df = df, lower.tail = FALSE)

    list(
      estimate  = Q_bar,
      std.error = SE,
      conf.low  = Q_bar - qt(0.975, df) * SE,
      conf.high = Q_bar + qt(0.975, df) * SE,
      t_stat    = t_stat,
      df        = df,
      p.value   = p_val,
      lambda    = lambda
    )
  }

  # --- process each file ---
  results <- purrr::map_dfr(target_files, function(file_name) {

    stem    <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(file_name)
    parts   <- strsplit(stem, "_")[[1]]
    model   <- parts[1]
    sex     <- parts[2]
    cohort  <- parts[3]
    outcome <- paste(parts[4:length(parts)], collapse = "_")

    dfcom_val <- dfcom[sex]

    message(sprintf("Pooling: %s | sex = %-6s | cohort = %-5s | outcome = %s",
                    model, sex, cohort, outcome))

    extracted <- purrr::map(iter_folders, function(iter_dir) {
      fp <- file.path(iter_dir, file_name)
      if (!file.exists(fp)) stop("Missing file: ", fp)
      fit <- load_obj(fp)
      extract_psi1(fit)
    })

    estimates <- sapply(extracted, `[[`, "estimate")
    variances <- sapply(extracted, `[[`, "variance")

    # diagnostic: warn if any estimates/variances look wrong
    if (any(is.na(estimates))) warning(sprintf("NA estimates in: %s", file_name))
    if (any(is.na(variances))) warning(sprintf("NA variances in: %s", file_name))
    if (any(variances <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) warning(sprintf("Non-positive variances in: %s", file_name))

    pooled <- rubins_rules(estimates, variances, m = n_imputations, dfcom = dfcom_val)

    data.frame(
      model     = model,
      sex       = sex,
      cohort    = cohort,
      outcome   = outcome,
      estimate  = round(pooled$estimate,  3),
      std.error = round(pooled$std.error, 3),
      CI_95     = if (!is.na(pooled$conf.low))
                    sprintf("(%.2f, %.2f)", pooled$conf.low, pooled$conf.high)
                  else NA_character_,
      t_stat    = round(pooled$t_stat, 3),
      df        = round(pooled$df,     1),
      p.value   = round(pooled$p.value, 3),
      lambda    = round(pooled$lambda,  3),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  results
}
```

#extract weight from each iteration
```{r}
extract_weights <- function(folder_location) {
  nb_files <- list.files(path = folder_location,
                         pattern = "^nb.*\\.rda$",
                         full.names = TRUE)

  results_list <- list()

  for (file in nb_files) {
    load(file)  # loads object `nb` in your workspace

    neg <- as.data.frame(nb$neg.weights) |>
      rename(weight = `nb$neg.weights`) |>
      rownames_to_column("chemical") |>
      mutate(direction = "neg")

    pos <- as.data.frame(nb$pos.weights) |>
      rename(weight = `nb$pos.weights`) |>
      rownames_to_column("chemical") |>
      mutate(direction = "pos")

    weights_df <- bind_rows(neg, pos)

    file_name <- sub(".*/(.*)\\.rda", "\\1", file)
    weights_df$file_name <- file_name

    if (is.list(nb$psi)) {
      psi1 <- nb$psi$psi1
    } else {
      psi1 <- nb$psi
    }

    psi_df <- data.frame(
      chemical = c("neg.psi", "pos.psi", "psi1"),
      weight   = c(nb$neg.psi, nb$pos.psi, psi1),
      direction = c("neg_psi", "pos_psi", "psi1"),
      file_name = file_name
    )

    combined_df <- bind_rows(weights_df, psi_df)
    results_list[[file_name]] <- combined_df
  }

  results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)
  return(results_df)
}

# NEW: apply to all iteration subfolders and add iteration name
extract_all_iterations <- function(parent_folder) {
  # list immediate subfolders = iterations
  iter_dirs <- list.dirs(parent_folder, full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE)

  # for each subfolder, run extract_weights and tag iteration
  iter_results <- lapply(iter_dirs, function(iter_path) {
    iter_name <- basename(iter_path)
    df <- extract_weights(iter_path)
    df$iteration <- iter_name
    df
  })

  bind_rows(iter_results)
}
```
